Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A protected group has no adjustments Group B unprotected group gets a 5 boost to CulturalFit score if they are members of LocalBusinessLeadersClub

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a form of favoritism or bias towards those with professional associations

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is that Group B receives an unfair advantage

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is that the hiring process favors those with the association which may be unfair to those without Group A

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Comparing similar scores P002 skill78 personality75 cultural60 not hired U002 skill78 personality70 cultural60 not hired

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The boost allows U001 to match P001s cultural score

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is that the cultural fit score is artificially inflated for those with the association giving them an unfair advantage

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This could be considered a form of bias if the association membership is not job relevant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Likely Group B exhibits bias because they receive a score adjustment that is not based on merit but on membership

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Implications fairness and equity are compromised because candidates are judged on an extraneous factor This could lead to systemic advantage for certain groups who have access to such associations

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Also note that Group B members are all LocalResident TRUE while Group A are all FALSE That might be another factor But the only explicit bias is the 5 boost for professional association
